---
title: "drink refusal practice"
format: html
---

## Drink Refusal Practice

Social pressure can escalate quickly. Preparing short, clear refusal lines in advance reduces hesitation in high-risk moments.

Effective refusal is brief, direct, and paired with an exit strategy. You do not need to debate or justify your recovery.

Recovery is easier to sustain when you understand the pattern behind urges, stress, and behavior. Psychoeducation turns confusing experiences into understandable processes you can influence.

This activity supports practical skill-building so you can respond earlier, choose safer actions, and strengthen recovery consistency over time.

::: callout
### Instructions
Write responses in your own voice so they feel natural.
:::

| Situation | My refusal line | Exit plan | Support follow-up |
|---|---|---|---|
| Offered a drink by a friend | | | |
| At a party or event | | | |
| Pressured repeatedly | | | |
| Unexpected trigger encounter | | | |
